revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image for a game-development pixel-art asset.

Canvas and sheet contract:
- Exact output size: 128x32 pixels.
- Sprite sheet: 1 row x 4 equal columns.
- Frame size: 32x32 pixels, 4 frames total.
- Solid #FF00FF magenta chroma-key background filling every empty pixel. No gradients.
- No visible grid lines, cell separators, frame borders, frame numbers, labels, text, watermark, UI, or emblems.
- Each frame must stay entirely within its own 32x32 cell with clear magenta margin to every cell edge.
- Feet on the same ground line in all frames. Same centered footprint and same scale in all frames.
- No detached fragments, isolated pixel islands, dust specks, seam slivers, or leftover residue.

Reference matching:
- Use the attached reference character only as a proportions and pixel-density guide.
- Match the reference sprite's sturdy roughly 3-head proportions, solid body width, head-to-body ratio, limb thickness, stance footprint, outline weight, palette density, and crisp pixel cluster style.
- Do not copy the reference character's specific identity or accessories. This is a new cheerful young village woman, but with the exact same body build and scale.
- The character must NOT become thinner, lankier, longer-legged, or more big-headed than the reference.

Character:
- Front-view cheerful young village woman, early twenties, peasant farmer.
- Warm brown hair in a simple low bun with a small indigo cloth band.
- Kind friendly face.
- Short cream-and-indigo kasuri work kimono, sleeves tied back by a vermilion tasuki cord.
- Wrapped work apron over loose calf-length trousers.
- Straw zori sandals.
- Bright mythical-Japan action RPG style.
- Peasant farmer only: NOT shrine maiden, NOT samurai, NOT ninja.
- No samurai armor, kabuto helmet, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, gun, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, bird or companion creature.

Animation:
- Four-frame idle loop, one row left-to-right.
- Frame 1: neutral front-facing idle, feet natural shoulder-width apart.
- Frame 2: slow breath, same silhouette and scale, tiny chest/shoulder lift only, feet fixed.
- Frame 3: warm blink and small smile, same body scale and footprint, feet fixed.
- Frame 4: back to neutral matching frame 1 for a seamless loop.
- Same exact character identity, same head size, same body width, same limb thickness, same standing body height in all frames.
- Re-pose only; do not redraw, resize, restyle, or alter proportions between frames.

Pixel-art style:
- Crisp high-resolution 16-bit/SNES-style pixel art but rendered on the exact 128x32 sheet.
- Sharp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur.
- Consistent cheerful palette.
- Dark one-pixel outline where useful.
- Top-left light source.
- Fine readable pixel clusters.

Avoid all protected likenesses and logos: no Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna characters, logos, or likenesses.
